summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened Resurrection Sunday by asking the congregation a foundational question: 'What are you looking for in life?' He observed that many people chase after things like money, success, relationships, and pleasure, only to find them ultimately meaningless, like 'chasing the wind.' Drawing from the story of King Solomon in Ecclesiastes, Pastor Travis explained that the world can never offer enough to satisfy the human heart. The true answer, he proclaimed, is found in the resurrected Jesus Christ. The core of the sermon was an invitation to 'try Jesus,' presenting four compelling reasons why He is the solution to our deepest longings. First, only Jesus offers a true fresh start and new life, as celebrated on Easter. Second, He provides a supernatural, lasting peace that the world cannot give. Third, Jesus is the source of genuine hope for our future, even when life doesn't turn out as we planned. Finally, and most importantly, Jesus offers a personal relationship with God, which is radically different from empty religion. Pastor Travis concluded with a powerful call to surrender, leading many in a prayer to begin a new life in Christ, emphasizing that in Jesus, we find the purpose, peace, and fulfillment we've been searching for.
mainPoints: Only Jesus Christ offers a true fresh start and new life, freeing us from our past.
Jesus provides a supernatural, lasting peace that surpasses understanding and guards our hearts.
Salvation is about entering a personal relationship with God, not practicing empty religion.

versesMentioned.text: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to look at the tomb. There was a violent earthquake, for an angel of the Lord came down from heaven and, going to the tomb, rolled back the stone and sat on it. His appearance was like lightning, and his clothes were as white as snow. The guards were so afraid of him that they shook and became like dead men. The angel said to the women, 'Do not be afraid, for I know that you are looking for Jesus, who was crucified. He is not here; he has risen, just as he said. Come and see the place where he lay. Then go quickly and tell his disciples: ‘He has risen from the dead and is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see him.’ Now I have told you.' So the women hurried away from the tomb, afraid yet filled with joy, and ran to tell his disciples. Suddenly Jesus met them. 'Greetings,' he said. They came to him, clasped his feet and worshiped him. Then Jesus said to them, 'Do not be afraid. Go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me.'
Generations come and generations go, but the earth remains forever.

Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! In his great mercy he has given us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead, and into an inheritance that can never perish, spoil or fade. This inheritance is kept in heaven for you.
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!
Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid.

Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
Trust in the LORD with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.
'For I know the plans I have for you,' declares the LORD, 'plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.'

This is the word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD: 'Go down to the potter’s house, and there I will give you my message.' So I went down to the potter’s house, and I saw him working at the wheel. But the pot he was shaping from the clay was marred in his hands; so the potter formed it into another pot, shaping it as seemed best to him.
The LORD is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
Jesus replied, 'Very truly I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born again.'
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

Monday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 (NIV) - 'Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!'
Monday.action: Identify one thing from your 'old' life (a habit, worry, or regret) and consciously choose to leave it with Jesus today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for making me new. Help me to walk in the reality of my fresh start today and not look back.

Tuesday.scripture: John 14:27 (NIV) - 'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives.'
Tuesday.action: When you feel stress or anxiety today, pause and say out loud: 'Jesus, I receive your peace.'
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to receive the peace only you can give. Guard my heart and mind from the chaos around me.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 3:5-6 (NIV) - 'Trust in the LORD with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding...'
Wednesday.action: In a situation where you are tempted to rely solely on your own logic, take a moment to pray and consciously choose to trust God's plan instead.
Wednesday.prayer: God, my understanding is limited. I choose to trust you with all my heart today, especially with the things I don't understand.

Thursday.scripture: Jeremiah 29:11 (NIV) - 'For I know the plans I have for you... plans to give you hope and a future.'
Thursday.action: Write down one area of your life that feels 'crushed' or hopeless. Next to it, write: 'My Potter is not finished with me.'
Thursday.prayer: Father, thank you that my story is not over. I place my hopes and my future in your hands, the master Potter.

Friday.scripture: John 3:16 (NIV) - 'For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son...'
Friday.action: Spend 5 minutes in quiet prayer today not asking for anything, but simply thanking God for His love and for the gift of a relationship with Him.
Friday.prayer: God, thank you for loving me so much. Help me to know you more deeply today, not just know about you.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 34:18 (NIV) - 'The LORD is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on the past week. Acknowledge one challenge you faced, and remind yourself that God was close to you in that moment.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being near me when I am hurting. Help me to feel your presence and comfort, especially when I feel broken or crushed.
